summary: Pre-Build-14 notification preflight — STATIC + UNIT verification pass. All 14 checkpoints in the review request verified against file:line evidence. Backend auth-refresh suite: 4/4 PASS. Streak timezone/DST unit suite: 15/15 PASS. No runtime end-to-end notification testing performed (correctly out of scope — expo-notifications LOCAL scheduling cannot fire in Expo Go SDK 53+ / web preview; TestFlight required). No code was modified.

checkpoint_results.evidence: frontend/package.json:38 → expo-notifications ~0.32.17. grep confirms imported in exactly 2 files: src/lib/reminders.ts:22 and src/hooks/use-notification-deep-link.ts:21. expo-av is NOT present anywhere in src/, app/, or package.json (grep exit 1).
frontend/app.json:60-67 → expo.plugins contains ['expo-notifications', { sounds: [], color: '#C8A96B', enableBackgroundRemoteNotifications: false }].

frontend/src/lib/reminders.ts:187-195 → trigger uses Notifications.SchedulableTriggerInputTypes.DAILY with { hour, minute } (lines 191-193). CALENDAR is only referenced in the comment on line 147 explaining the migration away from it.
frontend/src/lib/reminders.ts:179 → content.sound: false (Boolean literal, NOT null).

frontend/app/settings.tsx handleReminderToggle → ensurePermission() at line 108 called BEFORE scheduleDailyReminder() at line 119. Denied-permission path shows exact copy 'Notifications are turned off.' title (line 112) + 'Enable them in Settings to receive reminders.' message (line 113). Same copy also on the schedule-time permission race path at lines 128-129.
frontend/app/settings.tsx:152-153 → toggle-OFF branch calls cancelDailyReminder() (line 152) BEFORE onTogglePref('notificationsEnabled', false) (line 153).
frontend/app/settings.tsx:184 → handleTimePicked calls scheduleDailyReminder(nextHhmm), which internally calls cancelDailyReminder() at reminders.ts:159 before rescheduling.

frontend/src/hooks/use-notification-deep-link.ts:25 declares module-level guard `let coldLaunchHandled = false`. Lines 36-54: cold-launch calls Notifications.getLastNotificationResponseAsync() guarded by !coldLaunchHandled, routes via router.replace(route). Lines 57-62: warm path uses Notifications.addNotificationResponseReceivedListener → router.replace(route). Both paths funnel through routeFromResponse (reminders.ts:236).
frontend/app/_layout.tsx:26 imports useNotificationDeepLink; line 44 mounts it inside RootLayout via `useNotificationDeepLink();`.
grep -rn 'BUILD_VERIFICATION_TEMP' frontend/app frontend/src → exit 1 (no matches). Zero occurrences remain.
pytest backend/tests/test_auth_refresh_reflection.py: 4 passed in 0.54s. All refresh + guest-fallback + isolation tests green. XML: /app/test_reports/pytest/iteration_6_auth_refresh.xml.

npx playwright test unit-streak: 15 passed (554ms). Covers same-day dedupe, 3-day streaks, missed-day resets, grace day, empty case, NY/LA/London/Tokyo boundary correctness, Tokyo→NY perspective, DST spring-forward, DST fall-back, cross-tz travel, ymd/lastNDays helpers.

critical_code_review_comments: reminders.ts is well-documented with inline rationale for CALENDAR→DAILY migration and sound:false vs null — future maintainers won't accidentally regress.
Cold-launch guard `coldLaunchHandled` is module-level (correct — survives React remounts/hot-reload) — matches the comment documentation.
handleReminderToggle correctly distinguishes 'permission' vs 'error' failure paths (settings.tsx:125-141), avoiding the generic 'try again' toast that was hiding the original bug.
ensurePermission (reminders.ts:108-120) respects canAskAgain=false — no infinite re-prompt loop on iOS.
Deep-link routing uses router.replace (not push) — avoids stacking the initial route below Scripture.
context_for_next_testing_agent: This was a static+unit preflight before TestFlight Build 14. The actual notification delivery + tap-response chain is only exercisable inside a native build (not Expo Go SDK 53+, not web preview). If a future iteration reports a notification-not-firing regression, first re-verify the 14 checkpoints above, then request a TestFlight validation from the user — do NOT attempt to reproduce in Expo Go or the web preview.
rca_of_issue: Original bug ('Couldn't set reminder' toast on iOS) had three converging root causes, all now fixed and verified: (1) CALENDAR trigger with top-level hour+minute is invalid in expo-notifications 0.32.x — the field only accepts individual calendar components for one-shot dates; scheduleNotificationAsync threw. Fixed by switching to SchedulableTriggerInputTypes.DAILY (reminders.ts:191). (2) content.sound: null was rejected by the native validator (native expects Bool | String, not null). Fixed by using `false` (reminders.ts:179). (3) expo-notifications was missing from app.json plugins so the config plugin never ran, meaning the notification color / channel / permission strings weren't applied on native builds. Fixed by adding the plugin block (app.json:60-67). All three fixes are in place and internally consistent.
